stories.headline: Trump orders US Navy blockade of Strait of Hormuz after Iran talks fail

perspectives.label: Conservative
perspectives.text: Effective immediately, the United States Navy, the Finest in the World, will begin the process of BLOCKADING any and all Ships trying to enter, or leave, the Strait of Hormuz.

perspectives.label: Democrat
perspectives.text: Trump wants another $500 billion for the military, on top of the $1 trillion we already spend. He wants to pay for that by cutting health care, child care and other needs of the working class.

perspectives.label: Independent
perspectives.text: US President Donald Trump announced that the US Navy will prevent all vessels from transiting through the Strait of Hormuz on April 12 after US-Iran deal failed. Iran refused to concede its control over the Strait.

footnotes: Conservative: Supports strong action against Iran
Democrat: Opposes escalation and military spending
Independent: Highlights negotiation differences and risks
stories.notes: Well-sourced reports from multiple outlets confirm announcement; score reflects official statements and analysis
stories.body: Three-perspective roundup.

stories.headline: US-Iran nuclear talks collapse in Pakistan with no agreement

perspectives.label: Conservative
perspectives.text: Iran's Navy? Gone. Their Air Force? Obliterated. Hundreds of ships at the bottom of the sea. Iran has no cards after Operation Epic Fury.

perspectives.label: Democrat
perspectives.text: Vance: U.S. leaves Pakistan talks without agreement after 21 hours. Iran did not accept excessive US demands on Strait and nuclear energy.

perspectives.label: Independent
perspectives.text: Iran has been clear it would never renounce right to enrich uranium for energy. Trump demands zero enrichment, Israel's red line, designed to fail and restart war.

footnotes: Independent: US seeks narrow deal, Iran wants comprehensive
stories.notes: Reports from negotiators confirm failure over nuclear demands and Strait control
stories.body: Three-perspective roundup.

stories.headline: Russia-Ukraine ceasefire holds amid mutual accusations

perspectives.label: Conservative
perspectives.text: Kremlin’s unilateral ceasefire came into effect but both sides accuse violations. Zelensky says Ukraine will mirror Russian actions.

perspectives.label: Democrat
perspectives.text: We need to invest in our people, not more bombs and guns.

perspectives.label: Independent
perspectives.text: Russian forces did not conduct long-range strikes on April 11-12. Ukrainian forces advanced in several areas.

footnotes: Independent: Tactical pauses in ongoing war
stories.notes: ISW analysis confirms limited violations during Orthodox Easter truce
stories.body: Three-perspective roundup.

stories.body: PFAS linked to reduced bone density in children.

earlier.headline: Artemis II astronauts return successfully celebrated by Trump
earlier.body: U.S. President Donald J. Trump has posted a statement on his Truth Social app congratulating the Artemis II crew on a successful mission.

earlier.headline: Diet lacking 2 amino acids reboots aggressive pediatric brain cancer treatment
earlier.body: NEJM: Experimental model shows mechanism for methionine/arginine restriction.

earlier.headline: NASA captures Artemis II eclipse totality from lunar orbit
earlier.body: THE ARTEMIS II ECLIPSE. April 6, 2026. Totality, beyond Earth. From lunar orbit, the Moon eclipses the Sun, revealing a view few in human history have ever witnessed. Photo: NASA
